headline: Yen sinks to 40-year low, intervention risk rising
detail: A weak yen pressures global risk sentiment and can trigger sudden volatility if Japan intervenes - historically that causes fast moves in US equities. Tech and semiconductor names, which have heavy Asia supply-chain exposure, tend to feel it quickest. Your SMH, SOXL, and CRWD are all vulnerable to a sharp risk-off spike.
rec: No trade yet, but if yen intervention headlines hit intraday, SOXL is your most dangerous position - be ready to cut it first given 3x leverage.

headline: Oil posts largest quarterly drop in 6 years
detail: Falling oil is usually good for tech (lower input costs, consumer spending shifts), but a drop this size signals demand fear, which can flip to broad risk-off quickly. It also suggests macro growth worries are real, not just energy-specific. Your concentrated tech/chip book isn't directly in energy but gets hit if growth fears spread.
rec: No action needed now, but watch if SPY or QQQ start rolling - that's your signal the demand fear is contagious, and you'd want to respect your $705 SPY stop closely.

headline: Blue Origin pivots after explosion, targets end of 2026
detail: Relevant context for SPCX given SpaceX is a direct competitor in commercial launch. Blue Origin delays and setbacks reinforce SpaceX's dominance in the space launch market, which is modestly positive for SPCX's underlying narrative. However, SPCX is a brand-new IPO with limited data - one competitor's stumble doesn't change valuation risk.
rec: Don't add SPCX on this - it's already in your book, it's new and volatile, and a competitor's bad news isn't a clean enough catalyst to size up a margin position in an untested IPO.

headline: Wall Street says sell Goldman, buy Capital One
detail: Financials rotation call - not directly relevant to your tech/chip book, but broad sector rotation out of financials into consumer credit names can signal defensive repositioning that sometimes precedes tech pullbacks. Worth noting as a macro mood indicator, not a direct threat to your holdings.
rec: Nothing to do here for your book - your only financials-adjacent exposure is SPY and QQQ, both holding fine above their stops.
